description: Filter out core @opentabs-dev platform packages from plugin search results in both the MCP server (side panel) and CLI. Currently, packages like @opentabs-dev/create-plugin, @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk, and @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools appear in search results because they have the 'opentabs-plugin' npm keyword, but they are not installable plugins.

userStories.title: Add PLATFORM_PACKAGES constant to shared package
userStories.description: Add a set of @opentabs-dev package names that are core platform packages (not plugins) to the shared constants, so both the MCP server and CLI can use it for filtering.
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/shared/src/constants.ts (add the constant)
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (re-export it)

Context:
The shared package already exports plugin-related constants like PLUGIN_PREFIX and resolvePluginPackageCandidates from constants.ts and re-exports them from index.ts.

Changes:
Add to platform/shared/src/constants.ts:
```ts
export const PLATFORM_PACKAGES = new Set([
  '@opentabs-dev/browser-extension',
  '@opentabs-dev/cli',
  '@opentabs-dev/create-plugin',
  '@opentabs-dev/mcp-server',
  '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk',
  '@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools',
  '@opentabs-dev/shared',
]);
```

Add PLATFORM_PACKAGES to the re-exports in platform/shared/src/index.ts alongside the existing PLUGIN_PREFIX export:
```ts
export { PLUGIN_PREFIX, PLATFORM_PACKAGES, ... } from './constants.js';
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify any existing exports or constants
- Place the new constant near the existing PLUGIN_PREFIX constant

userStories.title: Filter platform packages from MCP server npm search results
userStories.description: Filter out PLATFORM_PACKAGES from the searchNpmPlugins function in the MCP server so they don't appear in side panel search results.
acceptanceCriteria: searchNpmPlugins never returns results where name is in PLATFORM_PACKAGES
Filtering applies to both keyword search results and direct probe results
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts (add filter)

Context:
searchNpmPlugins (line 249) merges results from two sources: fetchAllPlugins (keyword search) and probeDirectPackage (exact match). Both can return platform packages. The merge loop starting at line 273 adds results to the `results` array.

Changes:
Import PLATFORM_PACKAGES from @opentabs-dev/shared. The existing import at line 13 already imports from shared:
```ts
import {
  atomicWrite,
  getConfigDir,
  getConfigPath,
  isValidPluginPackageName,
  isWindows,
  normalizePluginName,
  PLATFORM_PACKAGES,
  resolvePluginPackageCandidates,
} from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
```

In searchNpmPlugins, filter the `filtered` array after the query filter (around line 270):
```ts
filtered = filtered.filter(pkg => !PLATFORM_PACKAGES.has(pkg.name));
```

Also guard the probe result before adding it (around line 276):
```ts
if (probeResult && !PLATFORM_PACKAGES.has(probeResult.name)) {
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the function signature or return type
- Do NOT change any other logic in the function

userStories.title: Filter platform packages from CLI plugin search results
userStories.description: Filter out PLATFORM_PACKAGES from the CLI's npmSearchPlugins and handlePluginSearch functions so they don't appear in CLI search output.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs plugin search never shows @opentabs-dev/create-plugin, @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk, @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools, or any other PLATFORM_PACKAGES entry
Filtering applies to both keyword search results and direct probe results
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts (add filter)

Context:
The CLI has its own npm search implementation. handlePluginSearch (line 381) runs keyword search via npmSearchPlugins and direct probes via fetchPackageInfo in parallel. Results are merged into a `results` array (line 391-405) before display.

Changes:
Import PLATFORM_PACKAGES from @opentabs-dev/shared. The existing import at line 9 already imports from shared:
```ts
import {
  type ConfigSchema,
  type ConfigSettingDefinition,
  DEFAULT_HOST,
  isWindows,
  normalizePluginName,
  PLATFORM_PACKAGES,
  PLUGIN_PREFIX,
  resolvePluginPackageCandidates,
  TOOLS_FILENAME,
  toErrorMessage,
} from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
```

In handlePluginSearch, filter the final `results` array after the merge loop (after line 405, before the display logic at line 407):
```ts
const filtered = results.filter(r => !PLATFORM_PACKAGES.has(r.name));
```
Then use `filtered` instead of `results` in the remaining code (the `if (filtered.length === 0)` check, the display loop, etc.).

Alternatively, filter in the merge loop itself by adding `if (PLATFORM_PACKAGES.has(info.name)) continue;` in the direct results loop (line 394) and `if (PLATFORM_PACKAGES.has(pkg.name)) continue;` in the search results loop (line 400).

Constraints:
- Do NOT change npmSearchPlugins itself — it may be used elsewhere. Filter in handlePluginSearch.
- Do NOT change the display format or any other logic
